lines: Court coffee? It will not make you wiser, but it may make your mistakes faster.
New face. Pig and Swine logo. That combination usually means something is due.
states._comment: Fires once on first approach before Whimsy is met. Service register reads the firm logo as social context — no name exchange needed. once:true so the orientation line only plays once. Falls through to barista_b7_brew_prompt on the next interaction.

lines: Going to court today?

choices.text: What does this machine actually do?

states._comment: Tutorial gate. Reuses canonical flag coffee_retry_decision. 'Just passing through' writes accept so the player can skip without a dead-end. Code reads coffee_retry_decision == 'accept' to launch or bypass the mini-game. The machine-explainer state catches 'retry' on the next interaction.

lines: You press the right buttons at the right time, and the coffee becomes less legally alarming.
The machine grades you. I watch the grade. Clients used to do the same to their lawyers, but this is cheaper.
Brew coffee?
states._comment: Explainer state. First line is verbatim from minigames.txt §Narrative placement. Second line is the Beat 7 joke — clients grading lawyers is the institutional anchor. Both choices write 'accept' so Code can proceed uniformly.

lines: That cup has structure, balance, and suspicious appellate energy.
Even Whimsy looked up.
Two zloty.
states._comment: S-grade reaction when Whimsy is present. Place BEFORE canonical coffee_outcome_alert_plus in merged file. once:true. 'Whimsy looked up' is the observable social fact — the cynic registers quality without speaking to Cula directly.

lines: Solid. The court may now tolerate you.
Two zloty.
states._comment: A/B-grade with Whimsy present. Register-stable; voice sample from the canonical barista.json. once:true.

lines: Whimsy has a theory that bad coffee correlates with procedural overreach.
I have no opinion on that.
Two zloty. Drink it at the table.
states._comment: D/F-grade with Whimsy present. Joke: the barista's 'no opinion' is delivered while clearly having one. 'Procedural overreach' is the legal anchor. Place BEFORE canonical coffee_outcome_over_caffeinated in merged file. once:true.

lines: He's been at the corner table since eight. Orders one coffee, refills the cup with objections.
When you are ready.
states._comment: Ambient bridge after tutorial is complete, before Whimsy is recruited. Points the player toward Whimsy without naming the objective. 'Refills the cup with objections' is the joke — barista reads Whimsy's rhetorical mode as a service behavior.

lines: So the firm has another lawyer now.
The queue is longer already.
states._comment: Post-recruitment, no buff active. Dry social register. 'Queue is longer' is simultaneously literal and a light institutional observation about firm growth.

lines: Good coffee before court. That is a firm with standards.
Two zloty when you get back.
states._comment: Post-recruitment, A/B buff active. Warm service register held. 'Two zloty when you get back' is the joke — payment deferred like a procedural filing step.

lines: That was a very good cup. Try not to mention it to the office machine.
Professional rivalry is real.
states._comment: Post-recruitment, S-grade buff active. Coffee-machine-as-character running joke from minigames.txt — the café machine and the office machine are positioned as competitors. Keeps the gag alive without overloading.

idle_flavor: The queue does not pause for procedure.
Order at the counter. Confessions cost extra.
The table by the window is occupied by Whimsy's scarf and, sometimes, Whimsy.
Do not move the napkin pile. Last time someone did that, a rights memo fell out.
A queue is just a deadline with witnesses.
